stem: A cloud computing server cluster uses 3 parallel nodes for redundancy. The request served requires at least one node to function. node 1 has a reliability of 85%. node 2 has a reliability between 61% and 74% (uncertain due to limited operational data). node 3 has a reliability of 86%. Additionally, there is a common-cause failure probability (e.g., power outage, shared software bug) that could disable all nodes simultaneously, estimated between 3.0% and 6.5%. What is the overall probability that the request served?

ground_truth.answer: P(request served) ranges from 0.9273 to 0.9647. Point estimate: 0.9456.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 nodes fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.15 × 0.39 × 0.14 = 0.0082. P(works) = 0.9918.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.15 × 0.26 × 0.14 = 0.0055. P(works) = 0.9945.
With common-cause failure (3.0%-6.5%): P(works) = (1-P_cc) × P(independent works). Range: [0.9273, 0.9647].
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
Ignoring common-cause failures that can disable all paths simultaneously
